title: Refactor: extract shared project_root validation and parametrize targeted tests
analysis:
Part 1 (shared validation helper) is ALREADY COMPLETE: require_project_root() and validate_project_root() already exist in fused_memory/utils/validation.py. Both targeted.py (line 20: import, lines 86+352: usage) and tools.py (line 14: import, 11 usage sites) already delegate to the shared module. Identity tests in test_tools_validation.py and unit tests in test_validation.py already verify this.

Part 2 (parametrize tests) is TODO: Six structurally identical rejection tests at test_targeted.py:449-515 (3 for reconcile_task × 3 bad values, 3 for reconcile_bulk_tasks × 3 bad values) should collapse into 2 @pytest.mark.parametrize tests. The error message from require_project_root includes repr(project_root), so match patterns can include the specific bad value for more precise assertions.

Part 3 (journal gap) is TODO: In reconcile_task(), require_project_root(project_root) at line 86 fires BEFORE journal.start_run(run) at line 99, so validation failures leave no audit trail. Fix: move run creation + journal.start_run() before validation, then catch ValueError inside the try block. Add a specific except ValueError clause that journals 'failed' and re-raises (preserving the existing API contract where ValueError propagates to callers like TaskInterceptor._on_reconciliation_done). reconcile_bulk_tasks() has no journal interaction at all, so no journal gap to fix there.

steps.description: Replace 6 individual validation tests with 2 parametrized tests: test_reconcile_task_rejects_bad_project_root and test_reconcile_bulk_rejects_bad_project_root, each parametrized with @pytest.mark.parametrize('project_root', ['', 'dark_factory', '.']). Use re.escape(repr(project_root)) in match pattern for precise error matching. Delete the 6 old tests (lines 449-515). Run tests to confirm all 6 test cases (3+3) still pass.

Write failing test test_reconcile_task_validation_error_leaves_journal_trace: call reconcile_task with project_root='' (expect ValueError), then query journal.get_recent_runs('test-project', limit=1) and assert exactly 1 run exists with status 'failed'. This test will FAIL because currently the ValueError fires before journal.start_run().

In reconcile_task(): (1) move run_id creation, ReconciliationRun construction, and journal.start_run() ABOVE the require_project_root() call; (2) move require_project_root() inside the existing try block (first statement); (3) add an 'except ValueError' clause before the existing 'except Exception' that calls journal.complete_run(run_id, 'failed') and re-raises. This journals validation failures while preserving the ValueError propagation contract.

Run full test suite for test_targeted.py to verify: (a) the 6 parametrized validation cases pass, (b) the new journal trace test passes, (c) all existing tests (done handler, blocked handler, bulk, fencing, etc.) remain green. No regressions.

design_decisions.decision: Part 1 (shared validation helper) is already implemented — no code changes needed
design_decisions.rationale: require_project_root() and validate_project_root() already exist in fused_memory/utils/validation.py and are imported by both targeted.py and tools.py. Identity tests in test_tools_validation.py and unit tests in test_validation.py already verify this. Attempting to re-extract would be a no-op.

design_decisions.decision: Re-raise ValueError after journaling instead of absorbing into error dict
design_decisions.rationale: The existing except Exception block returns {'error': str(e)} without raising. However, the TaskInterceptor._on_reconciliation_done callback relies on task.exception() to detect failures in fire-and-forget background tasks. Changing ValueError to an error dict would silently swallow validation failures at the interceptor layer. Re-raising preserves the existing API contract.

design_decisions.decision: Journal gap fix only applies to reconcile_task(), not reconcile_bulk_tasks()
design_decisions.rationale: reconcile_bulk_tasks() has no journal interaction at all (no start_run/complete_run calls). Adding journal support to bulk would be scope creep — that's a separate enhancement if desired.

design_decisions.decision: Use re.escape(repr(project_root)) in parametrized test match patterns
design_decisions.rationale: The error message from require_project_root includes f'{project_root!r}' so matching the specific repr ensures each parametrized case validates the correct bad value appears in the error, not just the generic 'absolute path' substring. The '.' case contains a regex metacharacter, so re.escape is necessary.

reuse.how: Already imported and used in targeted.py. No changes needed — the shared helper extraction (Part 1) was completed in a prior task.
Already imported and used in tools.py. The dict-returning variant is the foundation for the raise-on-error require_project_root() wrapper.
Used in test_on_task_done_logs_run_actions to query runs after reconcile_task. Reuse the same pattern in the new journal gap test.

reuse.what: Existing reconciler fixture and mock setup
reuse.how: The reconciler fixture (with journal, mock_memory_service, mock_taskmaster, config, mock_event_buffer) provides everything needed for the new tests.
The injection vector parametrize tests demonstrate the project's convention for parametrized validation tests. Follow the same style.
